summary: The infant exhibits signs indicative of multifocal clonic seizures due to its bilaterally symmetric but independent jerking movements involving arms and occasionally affecting the head, which aligns better with widespread discharges across different areas of an underdeveloped nervous system characteristic for this age group. This pattern contrasts sharply against localized seizure types given no clear dominance side was observed nor any specific focus noted beyond general limb involvements suggesting a broader distribution typical among young infants experiencing such episodes post-intubation stressors potentially exacerbating neurological excitability issues commonly seen at birth transition phases marked
